title: Japan's Parliament approves reform of the Imperial Household Law to ensure succession through adoption of males from collateral branches
summary: The reform allows princesses to retain their status after marriage and authorizes the adoption of men from the former imperial house to ensure dynastic continuity.

title: Nikkei index suffers historic drop of over 4,000 points amid tech sector crisis
summary: The session, with a record intraday decline, reflects the impact of uncertainty over AI and the massive sell-off of semiconductors.

title: Japan enacts law penalizing destruction of the national flag
summary: The rule, approved with broad parliamentary consensus, punishes acts that damage the flag causing public nuisance.

title: US completes a week of attacks in Iran as Tehran threatens to block the Strait of Hormuz
summary: The military escalation boosts shipping stocks and heightens global concern over energy supply.

title: Argentina and Spain clash in World Cup final amid fan euphoria
summary: The match, followed with passion in Japan, especially in Shibuya, comes accompanied by controversies such as the Falklands banner and the presence of Trump.

title: Nurse accused of introducing feces into intravenous drip that caused death of a patient
summary: The accused denies the charges, but the autopsy revealed fecal contamination in the drip that led to cardiac arrest.

title: Spray attack at Osaka junior high school leaves at least 23 students with irritation
summary: An unknown person sprayed an aerosol inside the school, causing mild symptoms in students who were treated immediately.

title: Naked corpse found in Kagoshima river may be that of missing 5-year-old boy
summary: The body was found 700 meters downstream from the family hot springs where the child was last seen.

title: Court orders sending minor as alleged perpetrator of robbery-murder in Tochigi
summary: The family court decides that the teenager be prosecuted by the public prosecutor's office after the violent incident.

title: Chinese state media publishes AI-generated video that offends Philippines by portraying them as monkeys
summary: The international community criticizes the racist content, while Beijing defends artistic freedom of expression.

title: Labour's Andy Burnham becomes UK prime minister without a vote
summary: Burnham's unopposed election as Labour leader makes him prime minister with the challenge of revitalizing the party.

title: Trump administration reduces duration of visas for foreign students and journalists
summary: The new measures, which seek stricter immigration control, limit the validity of study visas to four years.

title: Japan passes judicial review law that limits prosecution appeals and allows new evidence
summary: The reform introduces a system of order for evidence submission and establishes periodic reviews of the criminal process to prevent judicial errors.

title: Kyoto's Gion Festival holds traditional float procession with 23 decorated floats
summary: Despite the heat, thousands of spectators watch the parade on the streets of the city's historic center.

title: US court orders Kioxia to pay 37.1 billion yen for violating flash memory patents
summary: The ruling affects the Japanese firm amid growing competition in the AI chip market.

title: Israeli parliament dissolves, snap elections called for October
summary: The political crisis casts doubt on Benjamin Netanyahu's continuation as prime minister after the elections.

title: Kim Jong Un meets with Chinese senior official Wang Huning in Beijing to strengthen alliance
summary: Both countries commemorate 65 years of cooperation and agree to deepen strategic coordination.

title: France joins European trend by legalizing assisted suicide under medical conditions
summary: The law allows terminally ill patients to receive medical help to die, generating ethical debate in society.

title: Australia strengthens security cooperation with Pacific island states against Chinese influence
summary: The new agreement includes defense and maritime surveillance measures to contain Beijing's regional expansion.

title: Nobel laureate in Medicine Susumu Tonegawa dies at 85, pioneer in the study of the immune system
summary: The Japanese scientist, first Nobel winner for his work on antibody diversity, leaves an indelible legacy in science.
